core_mechanism: anomalous (superdiffusive) transport governed by a fractional-Laplacian nonlocal diffusion equation, derived as the anomalous-scaling hydrodynamic limit (t ~ n^{3/2}) of a microscopic stochastic exchange process, with nonlocal boundary operators at the domain endpoints
brief_reason: The abstract proves that the microscopic energy profile of a stochastic chain converges under superdiffusive time scaling to a temperature field solving a fractional heat equation whose generator is a fractional Laplacian with additional nonlocal boundary terms.
